Title: Gaming PC
Notes: Checked the PC and attempted to connect it to a monitor to access BIOS, but no display was observed and the system shuts down after a short time; based on troubleshooting, issue is suspected hardware (PSU/CPU overheating), advised to prioritize data recovery by removing the M.2 NVMe SSD and recovering files externally before further repair

Title: Gaming PC
Notes: The PC was shutting down automatically due to critical CPU overheating. Upon inspection, the internal components were found to be heavily dust-clogged, which restricted airflow and cooling performance. Additionally, the CPU liquid cooler (AIO) was not effectively transferring heat, and the thermal interface material between the CPU and cooler needs replacement. I have ordered some parts to fix that will be doing when parts arrive.

Title: Gaming PC
Notes: Root cause identified as severe dust buildup and degraded thermal compound causing CPU overheating and automatic shutdown. System was cleaned, CPU cooler re-seated, and new thermal paste applied. CPU temperatures are now stable (~60–62 °C under idle/BIOS). System is operating within safe thermal limits.

Title: Heather need of urgent assist regarding a file she needs
Notes:
Summary:
Received a call from Heather at KES regarding an issue with a missing file. She had accidentally overwritten the file and was seeking to recover the previous version. After attempting to recover the file through M365 and iCloud, and checking on iMac, the older version could not be found.

Actions Taken:

  1. Connected to Heather’s Mac and searched for the file, but was unable to locate the oldest version due to system limitations.

  2. Attempted recovery using M365 and iCloud version history, but was unsuccessful.

  3. Discovered that Heather has two Mac devices, and one of them was powered off.

  4. Stopped all internet connections on both devices.

  5. Powered on the old Mac device that had the old version of the file.

  6. Recovered the file with a new naming convention and made a copy.

  7. Re-enabled the internet connection and allowed iCloud sync to complete.

  8. Successfully recovered the old file version.

Next Steps:

  • No further steps required as the file recovery was successful.

Status: Issue resolved and file recovery completed; ticket closed.
